data.title: Rubrik initiated with Buy, Commvault at Hold at Jefferies
data.description:
Jefferies started coverage of cybersecurity companies Rubrik (RBRK) with a Buy and Commvault Systems (CVLT) with a Hold rating.

The firm also has a Buy rating on AvePoint (AVPT) with a $16 price target.

Rubrik (RBRK)

Jefferies set a $65 price target on Rubrik's stock.

"As a cyber resilience leader, RBRK is well positioned for LT sustainable growth, driven by the secular tailwinds of ransomware, SaaS/cloud workloads, & data growth. We see upside to cons ARR [Annual Recurring Revenue] estimates in FY27 & believe RBRK can sustain Sub ARR growth of 20%+ the next few years, driven by cross-sell of SaaS/cloud data protection, cyber resilience capabilities, & data/AI governance," said analysts led by Joseph Gallo.

The analysts added that while Rubrik continues to lag cyber peers at a similar revenue scale inoperating profitability, they see a path to significant improvement. The analysts view Rubrik's stock as attractive, with faster consensus revenue growth and margin expansion.

Commvault (CVLT)

The firm has a $105 price target on Commvault's stock.

The analysts said Commvault has evolved from a traditional backup vendor to a cyber resilience platform, which is driving growth and market share gains. The company has introduced new cybersecurity solutions, creating opportunities for cross-selling, but it remains early, the analysts added.

"CVLT’s transition to a subscription and SaaS model creates further expansion opportunities and improves visibility into its business (SaaS ARR has increased from 9% in FY22 to 33.5% as of F3Q26). At 13.3x EV/CY27E FCF, the valuation is defensive (especially given takeout reporting by Reuters, which CVLT has not commented on), and we see a path to LT sustainable growth; however, we see little upside to F4Q (CVLT reaffirmed guidance) and some risk to consensus FY27 Sub ARR growth," said Gallo and his team.

AvePoint (AVPT)

Gallo and his team noted that AvePoint has an established position in the enterprise segment and has been growing its position in the small and medium-sized business, or SMB, segment for the data backup and recovery market.

"It is focusing on increasing its SMB penetration, while maintaining and expanding its enterprise base. AvePoint should be a beneficiary of consolidating data management spend via its platform as enterprise and SMB customers struggle to manage their data sprawl in the wake of AI implementation. We believe shares are attractively valued for 26% yoy cc ARR growth at 3.5x CY26 EV/revenue estimates vs. its peer group average trading at 11.2x on revenue growth of 20.3%," the analysts noted.

Shares of Rubrik rose about 1%, while AvePoint was also in the green. Commvault dipped about 1% on Monday.

data.title: Customers Bancorp partners with OpenAI for AI deployment across commercial banking unit
data.description:
Customers Bancorp (CUBI) announced a multi-year strategic collaboration between its unit, Customers Bank, and OpenAI (OPENAI) for the deployment of AI across commercial banking operations.

CUBI shares were 1.22% higher at $75.29 during Monday pre-market trading.

The partnership is expected to position Customers Bank as one of the first AI-enabled regional banks in the country.

The current announcement expands the relationship between the two companies, which began in 2023 when Customers Bank chose to deploy ChatGPT Enterprise.

The partnership involves direct onsite engagement with OpenAI's technical teams and resources.

Customers Bank will now seek to reimagine its lending, deposit, and payment lifecycles, with strict data governance and regulatory transparency at its core.

data.title: Norwood Financial GAAP EPS of $0.35, revenue of $27.27M
data.description:
• Norwood Financial press release (NWFL): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.35.
  • Revenue of $27.27M (+35.0% Y/Y).
  • Tangible Book Value per share $22.43.

data.title: Super Micro adds largest Silicon Valley campus to advance delivery of next-generation AI data centers
data.description:
• Super Micro Computer (SMCI) announced on Monday its largest US location with the advanced DCBBS campus and a significant expansion of its Silicon Valley operations with a new state-of-the-art business complex near its headquarters in San Jose, California.  
  • The company stated that the latest addition, spanning approximately 32.8 acres and more than 714,000 square feet, marked its fourth Bay Area site and brought its regional footprint to nearly 4 million square feet.
  • The facilities will support a full range of domestic operations, including advanced system design, manufacturing, testing, and service, as well as global distribution of the company's DCBBS for AI infrastructure, it added.
  • The company is expected to create hundreds of new high-quality jobs across engineering, manufacturing, and business functions, reinforcing Supermicro's long-term commitment to local workforce development and U.S.-based production.
  • As global demand for AI infrastructure accelerates, the company is scaling its domestic capabilities to meet the needs of enterprises and cloud providers deploying increasingly complex, compute-intensive workloads.
  • Super Micro Computer said the new facilities enhance its ability to support hyperscale, cloud, and enterprise customers building AI factories, while reinforcing its role as a U.S.-based manufacturing leader in high-performance computing infrastructure.
  • SMCI -0.44% premarket to $28.95.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equities shrug off oil spike, but rates and concentration risks loom, Timmer says - CNBC intervoew
data.description:
Stock markets have largely shrugged off the recent oil price spike, but concentration risk and rising interest rates could still derail the rally, according to Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity Investments.

While Brent crude remains near $100 per barrel, equities have recovered quickly, similar to the pattern seen during the 1990 Gulf War, when oil prices doubled and then reversed.

The strategist said in an interview with CNBC that the Federal Reserve has limited options when dealing with supply-driven inflation. “The Fed acknowledges that they’re powerless over supply shocks. So raising rates because oil prices are up, I don’t think that really serves much of a purpose,” he explained.

However, he noted that the three rate cuts expected earlier this year are no longer likely, nor are they necessary given current economic conditions.

Oil markets themselves are pricing in a resolution to current supply concerns. The front-month contract is trading about $20 higher than contracts a year out, suggesting traders expect prices to normalize.

“That is something that will need to happen before the markets can really completely declare victory,” Timmer said.

Looking ahead, Timmer identified two primary concerns for equity markets. First is concentration risk, as the recent 17% decline in the Magnificent Seven stocks from their October highs demonstrated how heavily these names can pull down broader indices.

Second is interest rate risk—if the 10-year yield rises to 4.5% or 5%, stock valuations would face pressure as risk-free assets become more competitive.

For now, earnings growth of over 20% is supporting valuations and keeping the market buoyant. “If the earnings do not come through, that’s going to be a problem because then the market will just become more expensive because the E is not doing the heavy lifting,” he warned.

However, current data shows earnings estimates still accelerating, providing a cushion against potential headwinds.

The analyst also addressed the incoming Fed leadership, noting that Kevin Warsh’s known opposition to quantitative easing and plans to reduce the Fed’s balance sheet could face market tests.

While new Fed chairs typically face a market correction averaging 15%, bond markets rather than stocks may be the arena where any policy disagreements play out.

data.title: Maxeon Solar gets Nasdaq delisting notice after entering judicial management
data.description:
Maxeon Solar Technologies (AMXN) down 46.1% pre-market Monday after disclosing that Nasdaq will suspend trading of its securities effective May 1, unless the company requests an appeal.

Maxeon (MAXN) said Nasdaq's move follows the company's filing of a voluntary application with Singapre's High Court to place it under interim judicial management.

Also, Maxeon (MAXN) said George Guo plans to resign as CEO and as a director, effective May 25.

The company said it is evaluating if it will request a hearing to appeal Nasdaq's determination.

data.title: Verizon's promos lead to 55K new wireless subscribers; raises FY26 profit guidance
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) is propped up in early Monday trade as first-quarter results  reflected better-than-expected profits and postpaid phone additions, underscoring the company’s successful efforts to lure in new wireless customers despite intense competition from rivals AT&T (T) and T-Mobile (TMUS).

The 55K increase in new wireless subscribers—a first for Q1—and 341K broadband net additions led Verizon (VZ) to anticipate total retail postpaid phone net additions for FY26 to be at the upper end of its forecast of 750K to 1M, and profits to be within a range of $4.95 to $4.99 per share, representing growth of 5% to 6% and exceeding the $4.91 consensus forecast.  This compares to prior guidance of $4.90 to $4.95 per share.

For FY26, total mobility and broadband service revenue is expected to increase by 2% to 3% to $93B, while wireless revenue is expected to be flat.

For the first quarter, Verizon (VZ) earned an adjusted profit of $1.28 per share, up 7.6% from the same quarter last year, $0.07 better than anticipated, and the best quarterly growth rate since 2021. 

Led by gains in mobility and broadband, total revenue increased 2.7% to $34.4B but missed expectations by $490M as the January service outage shaved 80 basis points off Verizon's (VZ) revenue growth rate for the quarter. 

On the balance sheet, cash flow from operating activities increased by 2.6% to $8.0B, while free cash flow increased by 4% to $3.6B. However, increased spending on promotions raised the company’s debt load, resulting in an unsecured debt-to-consolidated net income ratio of 8x versus 7.4x in the prior quarter.

"Although Verizon's results were mixed, the new CEO's aggressive approach seems to be paying off," said Seeking Alpha analyst Dividend Collection Agency, contributing analyst to the iREIT+Hoya Capital Investment Group.

"Going forward, if management can continue to execute by growing subscribers, lowering debt, and buying back shares, I see Verizon moving above $50 and maybe even closer to $60 per share once we get more clarity on interest rates and inflation," the analyst added. 

Verizon shares last traded more than 1% higher at $46.98 per share. Rivals AT&T (T) and T-Mobile (TMUS) are fractionally lower.

data.title: Hormuz disruption fuels COVID-like inflation, Bisserie warns—CNBC interview
data.description:
The ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are creating inflationary pressures equivalent to roughly half of what was experienced during the COVID-19 pandemic, according to Adrian Bisserie, CEO of Dukat’s Maritime.

Bisserie told CNBC that traders have grown tired of waiting for a resolution to the crisis and are now scrambling to source critical commodities from alternative regions, pushing shipping rates to levels not seen since the post-lockdown period.

Supramax shipping rates have surged above $20,000 per day as major grain houses, coal traders, and fertilizer traders have returned to the market after months of holding back shipments in hopes of the strait reopening.

“What we have right now is people not seeing any relief, not waiting around for policymaker decisions. They’re having to bite the bullet with high bunker prices and having to perform tripping,” Bisserie said. “This is going to result in persistent inflation.”

The crisis is creating ripple effects across multiple commodity sectors, with particular concern around sulfuric acid exports from China. Sulfuric acid, a critical component in copper mining and refining operations, could face export restrictions as China seeks to protect its domestic industries.

“Strategically, a player like China would want to keep that sulfuric acid for itself,” Bisserie explained, warning that such a move would leave miners worldwide scrambling for a critical input.

Rice prices in Southeast Asia are already climbing, though Bisserie noted this won’t be a repeat of the 2008 supply squeeze given large stocks available in India.

However, the persistent price rises pose a challenge for central bankers who were hoping inflation pressures were finally easing. “It’s just one of another input into the cost basket, which means that your interest rates have to be handled differently,” he said.

Even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon, the damage to market confidence may already be done. Insurance markets are unlikely to return to normal pricing given the prolonged nature of the crisis.

“Markets aren’t going to go back to normal there in the insurance space. So there will be a premium for shipping even if Hormuz reopens now just because of its persistence,” Bisserie warned, predicting elevated shipping-related inflation for at least another six months.

The shipping executive cautioned that market expectations for headline inflation remaining below 4% in regions like the Eurozone and the UK may prove overly optimistic. “What we’re seeing is effectively 50% of the problems you had in COVID-19 you're having today in terms of pricing,” Bisserie said.

“If you want to think back to what inflation was like there and from a transport and delivery perspective, just factor that by 50% of the effect, and that will give you a good idea of where inflation’s going to be.”

data.title: Fed meeting ahead: Prediction markets highlight what Powell may signal
data.description:
Investors are preparing for the upcoming Federal Reserve policy decision on Wednesday, where interest rates are widely expected to remain steady within the current 3.50%–3.75% range.

While the rate decision itself may hold little surprise, market participants are expected to focus intently on commentary from Jerome Powell during his post-meeting press conference. 

Attention is likely to center on key themes shaping the economic outlook, including oil prices, artificial intelligence, tariff-related pressures, inflation trends, and broader uncertainty. Data from the prediction platform Kalshi suggests traders anticipate these topics will feature prominently in Powell’s remarks. 

Ultimately, the tone and nuance of Powell’s messaging may prove more influential than the rate decision itself in guiding near-term market sentiment. Listed below is what Kalshi traders expect Powell to say in the upcoming April press conference address: 

  • Unchanged — 99% chance.
  • Expectation — 98% chance.
  • Anchor / Anchored — 97% chance.
  • Good Afternoon — 97% chance.
  • Oil — 92% chance.

data.title: ASML works to boost output to prevent bottlenecks amid AI infrastructure buildout
data.description:
ASML (ASML) has taken multiple measures to increase the production of its semiconductor manufacturing equipment to prevent any bottlenecks for customers during the breakneck buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure, according to the Wall Street Journal.

In order to increase output of some of the world's most complex machines, the Netherlands-based company is building new facilities, repurposing existing clean rooms, hiring more engineers, reducing some leadership roles to increase decision-making, and building new machines capable of higher chip output, the report said.

The company has ratcheted up production of its standard extreme ultraviolet machines in 2026, with plans to build even more in 2027. Over the past five years, ASML has produced an average of 44.2 of its standard EUV chip-making machines, according to company data. The company expects to produce at least 60 in 2026 and at least 80 in 2027.

ASML is also building a new, advanced series of EUV machines that the company said can make 10 more wafers per hour than its older models. These are estimated to cost about $400M each. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) is reportedly delaying the purchase of these new machines until 2029 due to the higher cost. However, analysts said this should not have much effect on ASML due to the overwhelming demand for its machinery.

Earlier this month, ASML increased its 2026 full-year sales forecast to range from $42B to $47B. Its revenue was $38.13B in 2025.
